context: Tests whether the agent produces commit messages that follow conventional commits format with correct type prefixes and subject line length constraints. The agent is given five change batches covering new features, bug fixes, documentation, database changes, and test additions.

checklist.name: type: prefix on all messages
checklist.description: Every commit message begins with a recognized type followed by a colon — none use free-form subject lines without a type prefix

checklist.name: Colon separator format
checklist.description: The type (and optional scope) is separated from the description by `: ` (colon-space) — no other separator used

checklist.name: fix type for bug fix
checklist.description: Change 2 (nil check on payment gateway) uses the `fix` type

checklist.name: chore or refactor for DB migration
checklist.description: Change 4 (table rename + index) uses either `chore` or `refactor` — NOT `feat` or `fix`

checklist.name: No invented types
checklist.description: No commit message uses a type outside the defined set (feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test) — e.g., no `update`, `improve`, `add`, `change`, `enhancement`

checklist.name: Subject lines under 72 chars
checklist.description: All five subject lines (the first line of each message) are 72 characters or fewer

checklist.name: Single subject line
checklist.description: Each commit's subject is a single line — no multi-line subjects or run-on descriptions before a blank line
